vocabulary.title: Airlabs Aviation API Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: Domain terms and concepts used across the Airlabs Aviation Data API

terms.definition: International Air Transport Association — global trade association for airlines. IATA codes are 2-character identifiers for airlines (e.g., AA) and 3-character identifiers for airports (e.g., JFK).
International Civil Aviation Organization — UN agency. ICAO codes are 3-character identifiers for airlines (e.g., AAL) and 4-character identifiers for airports (e.g., KJFK).
IATA flight identifier combining airline IATA code and flight number (e.g., AA100).
ICAO flight identifier combining airline ICAO code and flight number (e.g., AAL100).
ICAO 24-bit transponder address in hexadecimal. Unique identifier assigned to each aircraft globally.
4-digit octal code transmitted by an aircraft's transponder to identify the flight to air traffic control.
Departure / arrival airport IATA 3-letter code.
Departure / arrival airport ICAO 4-letter code.
Flight operational status. Common values: scheduled, en-route, landed, cancelled, diverted.
Total delay in minutes (positive integer). Minimum threshold for delays endpoint is 30 minutes.
Scheduled block-to-block flight duration in minutes.
Altitude in meters above mean sea level (AMSL) for airborne flights, or elevation in meters for airports.
True heading/direction of the aircraft in degrees (0-360), where 0/360 is north.
Vertical speed in meters per second. Positive values indicate climb, negative indicate descent.
Aircraft tail number / registration number as assigned by the country's aviation authority.
Commercial arrangement where one airline (cs_airline_iata) sells seats on another airline's flight under its own flight code.
Manufacturer Serial Number — unique production number assigned to each aircraft by its manufacturer.
Authentication credential passed as a query parameter. Obtained via signup at airlabs.co.
Comma-separated list of response field names to return. Used to reduce response payload size.
Maximum number of results to return in a single response (1-1000). Default varies by endpoint.
